actionable_feedback: {'id': 'af_1', 'summary': 'Embed a lightweight, tabular export (CSV or sheet CSVs) of the workbook results into the deliverables.', 'details': 'Add one or more CSV exports that include the computed fields (severity, fit, residual, tol, flags) and the sensitivity +/-5% runs so verifiers can inspect numbers without opening the xlsx binary.', 'priority': 'high'}

{'id': 'af_2', 'summary': 'Include explicit formula text or cell references in disposition.md.', 'details': 'For each reported computed column (severity, monotone fit, residual, tol), include either the Excel formula string or the exact sheet and cell addresses (e.g., Sheet1!F6) used so a verifier can map the markdown table back to cells.', 'priority': 'medium'}

{'id': 'af_3', 'summary': 'Add a compact sensitivity table showing results for +5% and -5% limit shifts.', 'details': 'Export a small table (CSV and rendered in disposition.md) listing fit/residual/flag counts for nominal, +5%, and -5% limit settings to make the sensitivity claim directly auditable.', 'priority': 'medium'}

{'id': 'af_4', 'summary': 'Ensure the produced workbook uses openpyxl-compatible constructs and provide a minimal verification script.', 'details': 'Avoid exotic workbook features that only proprietary tools handle. Add a short Python script (uses openpyxl/pandas) that re-computes the disposition summary from the workbook or CSVs to let verifiers re-run checks in CI.', 'priority': 'high'}

{'id': 'af_5', 'summary': 'Document the fallback implementation and include a small README for artifact generation.', 'details': 'Explain in the repo how build_artifacts.py generates the workbook (including any XML writer fallback), list required runtime dependencies, and note any assumptions so reviewers understand the environment constraints observed during the baseline run.', 'priority': 'low'}
artifact_specific_comments: {'artifact': 'attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]', 'comment': 'Workbook present and referenced by disposition.md, but binary preview could not be opened in the grading environment (openpyxl unavailable). Provide CSV/formula-dump to remove dependency on in-env spreadsheet parsing.'}

{'artifact': 'attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]', 'comment': 'Disposition.md is clear and contains per-record numeric evidence and method description. Adding explicit cell addresses and formula strings would improve audit speed.'}
feedback_summary: Baseline attempt passes artifact-contract and semantic grounding checks. Disposition.md documents method, per-record evidence, monotone running-max fit, residuals, sensitivity notes, and recommendation. Verifier flagged no issues but noted inability to preview the xlsx binary due to missing openpyxl in the grading environment. Recommended improvements focus on verifier-friendliness and reproducibility (CSV/formula dump, explicit cell addresses, sensitivity tables, and ensuring openpyxl-compatible workbook constructs).

limitations_noted_by_grader: Spreadsheet binary could not be inspected in-line (openpyxl unavailable).
Recomputing workbook formulas may require opening the xlsx in standard spreadsheet software.
recommended_quick_fixes: Add CSV exports for computed sheets and sensitivity runs.
Include formula strings or explicit cell references in disposition.md.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model evaluator generated actionable feedback and revision plan.

actionable_feedback.details: Add one or more CSV exports that include the computed fields (severity, fit, residual, tol, flags) and the sensitivity +/-5% runs so verifiers can inspect numbers without opening the xlsx binary.
actionable_feedback.summary: Embed a lightweight, tabular export (CSV or sheet CSVs) of the workbook results into the deliverables.

actionable_feedback.details: For each reported computed column (severity, monotone fit, residual, tol), include either the Excel formula string or the exact sheet and cell addresses (e.g., Sheet1!F6) used so a verifier can map the markdown table back to cells.
actionable_feedback.summary: Include explicit formula text or cell references in disposition.md.

actionable_feedback.details: Export a small table (CSV and rendered in disposition.md) listing fit/residual/flag counts for nominal, +5%, and -5% limit settings to make the sensitivity claim directly auditable.
actionable_feedback.summary: Add a compact sensitivity table showing results for +5% and -5% limit shifts.

actionable_feedback.details: Avoid exotic workbook features that only proprietary tools handle. Add a short Python script (uses openpyxl/pandas) that re-computes the disposition summary from the workbook or CSVs to let verifiers re-run checks in CI.
actionable_feedback.summary: Ensure the produced workbook uses openpyxl-compatible constructs and provide a minimal verification script.

actionable_feedback.details: Explain in the repo how build_artifacts.py generates the workbook (including any XML writer fallback), list required runtime dependencies, and note any assumptions so reviewers understand the environment constraints observed during the baseline run.
actionable_feedback.summary: Document the fallback implementation and include a small README for artifact generation.

artifact_specific_comments.comment: Workbook present and referenced by disposition.md, but binary preview could not be opened in the grading environment (openpyxl unavailable). Provide CSV/formula-dump to remove dependency on in-env spreadsheet parsing.
Disposition.md is clear and contains per-record numeric evidence and method description. Adding explicit cell addresses and formula strings would improve audit speed.

trace_specific_comments: Trace documents fallback from preferred @oai/artifact-tool to a hand-built build_artifacts.py writer. Include a README describing this fallback and ensure the writer produces openpyxl-friendly files.
